checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueNet=5035603530.30 (FY2011) vs SalesRevenueNet=5378550859.45 (FY2009) - revenue essentially flat/declining while goodwill rose ~39% over same span

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=2625859627.31 (FY2012) / SalesRevenueNet=4957303086.18 (FY2012) => gross margin ~53%, up from ~48.5% in FY2011 (GrossProfit=2443520028.26 / SalesRevenueNet=5035603530.30)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsSold=2331443458.86 (FY2012) vs ProductWarrantyAccrualClassifiedCurrent=68090755.49 (2012-12-29) => warranty reserve/COGS ratio fell from ~5.8% (2009) to ~2.9% (2012) while gross margin simultaneously rose (GrossProfit=2625859627.31 FY2012 vs GrossProfit=2636138682.68 FY2009)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: Multiple Form 15-12G (deregistration) and 8-A12G/A filings in 2010 (2010-05-04, 2010-06-28) followed by continued 10-Q/10-K filings thereafter, indicating a corporate reorganization not clearly explained in the provided data

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spanning FY2007 through Q1 2013 provided for revenue, COGS, gross profit, operating income, net income, cash flow (operating/investing/financing), receivables, inventory, goodwill, PP&E, and warranty/allowance accounts
evidence.location: Aggregate data set across multiple filings 2010-02-24 through 2013-05-08

top_signals: Product warranty accrual declined from ~5.8% to ~2.9% of COGS between 2009 and 2012 while gross margin simultaneously expanded, a pattern potentially consistent with reserve release supporting margin improvement (ProductWarrantyAccrualClassifiedCurrent=159558101.82 (2008-12-27) vs 68090755.49 (2012-12-29))

Goodwill grew ~39% (Goodwill=235602301.50 (2009-12-26) to 327620930.85 (2011-12-31)) while revenue was flat-to-declining (SalesRevenueNet=5378550859.45 (FY2009) vs 5035603530.30 (FY2011)), suggesting acquisition-driven balance sheet growth not yet reflected in top-line growth

Two 10-Q/A amendments (2009-09-01 and 2011-08-30) plus an unusual sequence of Form 15-12G/8-A12G/A deregistration-reregistration filings in 2010 indicate some filing irregularity, though not clearly tied to a restatement of financial results

Operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income across FY2009-FY2012 (e.g., NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1249961244.16 vs NetIncomeLoss=990124394.80 in FY2012), which is a reassuring quality-of-earnings signal that offsets some of the above concerns
